mnemonics: CINVL: Characters, Integers, Numerics, Vectors, Logicals. Pronounced like 'Synvul', the data wizard who uses these basic spells in R.
keyPoints: The five basic atomic data types are: character, integer, numeric (double), logical, and complex.
Vectors are the fundamental data structure and can only contain elements of the same type.
Use the class() or typeof() functions to check the data type of an object.
commonMistakes: Assuming a number is stored as an integer by default (it's usually a numeric/double).
Using a single equals sign `=` for assignment instead of the arrow `<-`, though both can work, the latter is more conventional in R.
Trying to mix data types in a vector, which causes coercion to the most flexible type (e.g., c(1, 'a') becomes a character vector).
